1в†’# Workflow Automation Guide
     2в†’
     3в†’**Version:** 4.0 (Consolidated)
     4в†’**Last Updated:** December 2025
     5в†’**Purpose:** Complete workflow compliance guide - checklists + automation tools
     6в†’
     7в†’---
     8в†’
     9в†’## Quick Navigation
    10в†’
    11в†’| Section | Description |
    12в†’|---------|-------------|
    13в†’| [Quick Checklists](#quick-checklists) | Pre-flight, implementation, completion |
    14в†’| [Automation Tools](#automation-tools) | Git hooks, templates, commands |
    15в†’| [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions) | Initial configuration |
    16в†’| [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) | Common issues and fixes |
    17в†’| [Customization](#customization) | Extend and modify tools |
    18в†’| [Best Practices](#best-practices) | Tips for success |
    19в†’
    20в†’---
    21в†’
    22в†’# PART 1: Quick Checklists
    23в†’
    24в†’## Pre-Flight Checklist
    25в†’
    26в†’### Before Starting ANY Task
    27в†’
    28в†’- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules and core philosophy)
    29в†’- [ ] **Sync with remote branch:**
    30в†’  ```bash
    31в†’  git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
    32в†’  # OR: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
    33в†’  ```
    34в†’- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template
    35в†’- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
    36в†’  - Problem statement
    37в†’  - Files to read
    38в†’  - Proposed changes
    39в†’  - Todo checklist
    40в†’- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool
    41в†’- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation
    42в†’
    43в†’---
    44в†’
    45в†’## During Implementation
    46в†’
    47в†’### As You Work
    48в†’
    49в†’- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item
    50в†’- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible**
    51в†’  - Impact minimal code
    52в†’  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
    53в†’  - Keep changes focused on the task
    54в†’- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes
    55в†’- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes
    56в†’- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles**
    57в†’
    58в†’---
    59в†’
    60в†’## Project-Type Checklists
    61в†’
    62в†’### For UI/Visual Changes
    63в†’
    64в†’- [ ] Screenshot current state before changes
    65в†’- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
    66в†’- [ ] Test in browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
    67в†’- [ ] Test responsiveness: 375px, 768px, 1024px+
    68в†’- [ ] Compare to baseline (visual diff)
    69в†’- [ ] Check animations work (60fps target)
    70в†’- [ ] Get user approval before committing
    71в†’
    72в†’### For Node.js/React/TypeScript Projects
    73в†’
    74в†’- [ ] Run tests: `npm run test`
    75в†’- [ ] Type check: `npm run type-check`
    76в†’- [ ] Lint check: `npm run lint`
    77в†’- [ ] Build check: `npm run build`
    78в†’- [ ] Verify bundle size acceptable
    79в†’
    80в†’### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)
    81в†’
    82в†’- [ ] Manual verification in browser
    83в†’- [ ] Check console for errors
    84в†’- [ ] Resize window (mobile/desktop)
    85в†’- [ ] Run Lighthouse audit in DevTools
    86в†’
    87в†’### For Automation/Scripts
    88в†’
    89в†’- [ ] Test with `DRY_RUN=true` first
    90в†’- [ ] Verify output matches expectations
    91в†’- [ ] Check validation rules work
    92в†’- [ ] Test edge cases
    93в†’- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes
    94в†’
    95в†’---
    96в†’
    97в†’## When Complete
    98в†’
    99в†’### Before Marking Work "Done"
   100в†’
   101в†’- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md`
   102в†’  - Summary of changes
   103в†’  - Files created/modified
   104в†’  - Testing status
   105в†’  - Issues encountered
   106в†’- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed
   107в†’- [ ] **Verify in light mode** (if applicable)
   108в†’- [ ] **Verify in dark mode** (if applicable)
   109в†’- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation** (accessibility)
   110в†’- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes**
   111в†’
   112в†’---
   113в†’
   114в†’## Git Commit Checklist
   115в†’
   116в†’### Before Creating Commit
   117в†’
   118в†’- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md`
   119в†’- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
   120в†’- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
   121в†’  ```
   122в†’  type: Short description (50 chars max)
   123в†’
   124в†’  - Bullet point details
   125в†’  - What changed and why
   126в†’
   127в†’  Generated with Claude Code
   128в†’  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
   129в†’  ```
   130в†’- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore
   131в†’
   132в†’---
   133в†’
   134в†’## Quick Reference Tables
   135в†’
   136в†’### Core Workflow Rules
   137в†’
   138в†’| # | Rule | Description |
   139в†’|---|------|-------------|
   140в†’| 1 | **Think в†’ Plan в†’ Write** | Create task file FIRST before coding |
   141в†’| 2 | **Plan has todo list** | Checklist with items to check off |
   142в†’| 3 | **Check before working** | Get user approval via ExitPlanMode |
   143в†’| 4 | **Mark tasks complete** | Update todos as you go |
   144в†’| 5 | **High-level explanations** | Don't overexplain every detail |
   145в†’| 6 | **Simple changes** | Minimal code impact |
   146в†’| 7 | **Add review section** | Document what changed when done |
   147в†’| 8 | **No lazy fixes** | Find root cause, no temporary patches |
   148в†’| 9 | **Simplicity is key** | Impact minimal code, avoid bugs |
   149в†’| 10 | **Update documentation** | Keep docs detailed, clear, clean |
   150в†’| 11 | **Sync with remote** | Start of EVERY session |
   151в†’| 12 | **Perfect foundation** | Build for 100% even if 1% complete |
   152в†’| 13 | **Complete logical units** | Never stop mid-implementation |
   153в†’| 14 | **Validate current date** | Check `<env>` section |
   154в†’| 15 | **Commit & Push at end** | Git add, commit with details, push |
   155в†’
   156в†’### Core Philosophy Principles
   157в†’
   158в†’| # | Principle | Description |
   159в†’|---|-----------|-------------|
   160в†’| 1 | **Correctness & Integrity** | No data loss. Security P0. |
   161в†’| 2 | **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
   162в†’| 3 | **Atomic Execution** | Small, reviewable steps. Explain WHY. |
   163в†’| 4 | **Maintainability** | Explicit > Clever. No magic. |
   164в†’| 5 | **Optimal Location** | Files must be in the most logical place. |
   165в†’| 6 | **Scalable Architecture** | Build primitives, not one-offs. |
   166в†’| 7 | **Root Cause Only** | Trace bugs to source. No band-aids. |
   167в†’| 8 | **Infinite Initiative** | Fix broken windows immediately. |
   168в†’
   169в†’---
   170в†’
   171в†’# PART 2: Automation Tools
   172в†’
   173в†’## Overview
   174в†’
   175в†’This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:
   176в†’
   177в†’- **Preventing commits** without task file updates
   178в†’- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation
   179в†’- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks
   180в†’- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
   181в†’
   182в†’**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
   183в†’
   184в†’### Supported Project Types
   185в†’
   186в†’| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
   187в†’|--------------|------------|-------------|
   188в†’| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
   189в†’| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
   190в†’| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
   191в†’| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |
   192в†’
   193в†’---
   194в†’
   195в†’## Tool 1: Git Pre-Commit Hook
   196в†’
   197в†’**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   198в†’**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
   199в†’**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
   200в†’
   201в†’**How it works:**
   202в†’1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
   203в†’2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
   204в†’3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
   205в†’4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
   206в†’
   207в†’**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
   208в†’```
   209в†’в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
   210в†’  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
   211в†’в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
   212в†’
   213в†’Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
   214в†’
   215в†’Required Action:
   216в†’  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
   217в†’  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
   218в†’  3. Retry commit: git commit
   219в†’```
   220в†’
   221в†’**Emergency Bypass:**
   222в†’```bash
   223в†’git commit --no-verify
   224в†’```
   225в†’**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies
   226в†’
   227в†’---
   228в†’
   229в†’## Tool 2: Task Template
   230в†’
   231в†’**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   232в†’**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
   233в†’**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7
   234в†’
   235в†’**Sections included:**
   236в†’- Overview and problem statement
   237в†’- Files to read
   238в†’- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
   239в†’- Todo checklist with time estimates
   240в†’- Design considerations
   241в†’- Implementation notes
   242в†’- Testing checklist
   243в†’- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)
   244в†’
   245в†’**Create new task:**
   246в†’```bash
   247в†’cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   248в†’```
   249в†’
   250в†’**Good naming examples:**
   251в†’- `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
   252в†’- `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
   253в†’- `tasks/refactor-state-management.md`
   254в†’
   255в†’**Bad naming examples:**
   256в†’- `tasks/todo.md`
   257в†’- `tasks/task1.md`
   258в†’- `tasks/stuff.md`
   259в†’
   260в†’---
   261в†’
   262в†’## Tool 3: Custom Commands
   263в†’
   264в†’**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
   265в†’
   266в†’### `/start-task` Command
   267в†’- Routes to appropriate workflow based on task type
   268в†’- Creates task files from templates
   269в†’- Checks existing todos
   270в†’- Initiates planning mode
   271в†’- Gets user approval before implementation
   272в†’
   273в†’### `/run` Command
   274в†’- Execute prompts with auto-archiving
   275в†’
   276в†’---
   277в†’
   278в†’# PART 3: Setup Instructions
   279в†’
   280в†’## File Structure
   281в†’
   282в†’```
   283в†’[project-root]/
   284в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .git/
   285в†’в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ hooks/
   286в†’в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   287в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
   288в†’в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                   (global instructions)
   289в†’в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.md                   (folder overview)
   290в†’в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docs/
   291в†’в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflow-automation.md  (this file)
   292в†’в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/
   293в†’в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ task-template.md        (task file template)
   294в†’в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ commands/
   295в†’в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ start-task.md           (custom commands)
   296в†’в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ skills/
   297в†’в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
   298в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tasks/                          (task files go here)
   299в†’в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                       (main workflow guide)
   300в†’в””в”Ђв”Ђ README.md                       (project documentation)
   301в†’```
   302в†’
   303в†’## Initial Setup
   304в†’
   305в†’**Verify setup:**
   306в†’```bash
   307в†’# Check git hook exists and is executable
   308в†’ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   309в†’
   310в†’# Check automation files exist
   311в†’ls -la .claude/
   312в†’
   313в†’# Make hook executable (if needed)
   314в†’chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   315в†’```
   316в†’
   317в†’## For New Developers
   318в†’
   319в†’1. **Clone repository**
   320в†’2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
   321в†’3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   322в†’4. **Test git hook:**
   323в†’   ```bash
   324в†’   # Make a small test change
   325в†’   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx
   326в†’
   327в†’   # Try to commit without staging task file
   328в†’   git add src/main.tsx
   329в†’   git commit -m "test"
   330в†’   # Should be BLOCKED
   331в†’
   332в†’   # Stage task file and retry
   333в†’   git add tasks/some-task.md
   334в†’   git commit -m "test"
   335в†’   # Should succeed
   336в†’
   337в†’   # Revert test
   338в†’   git reset HEAD~1
   339в†’   git checkout src/main.tsx
   340в†’   ```
   341в†’
   342в†’---
   343в†’
   344в†’# PART 4: Troubleshooting
   345в†’
   346в†’## Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   347в†’
   348в†’**Symptoms:** Can commit code without task file
   349в†’
   350в†’**Fixes:**
   351в†’
   352в†’```bash
   353в†’# Cause 1: Hook not executable
   354в†’chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   355в†’
   356в†’# Cause 2: Hook file doesn't exist
   357в†’ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   358в†’
   359в†’# Cause 3: Using --no-verify flag (don't do this)
   360в†’git commit  # Correct
   361в†’git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
   362в†’```
   363в†’
   364в†’## Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   365в†’
   366в†’**Symptoms:** Hook blocks even with task file staged
   367в†’
   368в†’**Fixes:**
   369в†’
   370в†’```bash
   371в†’# Cause 1: Task file in wrong location
   372в†’git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
   373в†’git add my-feature.md         # Wrong
   374в†’
   375в†’# Cause 2: Task file not staged
   376в†’git diff --cached --name-only  # Check what's staged
   377в†’
   378в†’# Cause 3: Wrong file extension
   379в†’git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct (.md)
   380в†’git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong
   381в†’```
   382в†’
   383в†’## Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   384в†’
   385в†’**Fixes:**
   386в†’```bash
   387в†’# Check if template exists
   388в†’ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   389в†’
   390в†’# Ensure you're in repository root
   391в†’pwd
   392в†’```
   393в†’
   394в†’---
   395в†’
   396в†’# PART 5: Customization
   397в†’
   398в†’## Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   399в†’
   400в†’**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   401в†’
   402в†’```bash
   403в†’# Add more directories to monitor
   404в†’CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   405в†’```
   406в†’
   407в†’## Customizing Task Template
   408в†’
   409в†’**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   410в†’
   411в†’Add sections like:
   412в†’```markdown
   413в†’## Security Checklist
   414в†’- [ ] Input validation added
   415в†’- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   416в†’- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   417в†’
   418в†’## Performance Checklist
   419в†’- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
   420в†’- [ ] No memory leaks
   421в†’- [ ] Animations 60fps
   422в†’
   423в†’## Accessibility Checklist
   424в†’- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
   425в†’- [ ] Screen reader compatible
   426в†’- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
   427в†’```
   428в†’
   429в†’## Adding New Custom Commands
   430в†’
   431в†’**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   432в†’
   433в†’```markdown
   434в†’---
   435в†’description: Your command description
   436в†’---
   437в†’
   438в†’# Command Content Here
   439в†’```
   440в†’
   441в†’---
   442в†’
   443в†’# PART 6: Best Practices
   444в†’
   445в†’## 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   446в†’
   447в†’```bash
   448в†’# View this file or use command
   449в†’/start-task
   450в†’```
   451в†’
   452в†’## 2. Create Task File FIRST
   453в†’
   454в†’```bash
   455в†’# CORRECT ORDER
   456в†’cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   457в†’# Fill out the task file
   458в†’# THEN start coding
   459в†’
   460в†’# WRONG ORDER
   461в†’# Start coding first
   462в†’# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   463в†’```
   464в†’
   465в†’## 3. Update Task File as You Work
   466в†’
   467в†’```markdown
   468в†’## Todo Checklist
   469в†’
   470в†’### Phase 1
   471в†’- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
   472в†’- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   473в†’- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked
   474в†’```
   475в†’
   476в†’## 4. Fill Review Section Immediately
   477в†’
   478в†’When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   479в†’- Summary of changes
   480в†’- Files created/modified
   481в†’- Testing status
   482в†’- Issues encountered
   483в†’
   484в†’## 5. Stage Task File with Code
   485в†’
   486в†’```bash
   487в†’# CORRECT - Stage both together
   488в†’git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   489в†’git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   490в†’git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   491в†’
   492в†’# WRONG - Hook will block
   493в†’git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   494в†’git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   495в†’```
   496в†’
   497в†’## 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   498в†’
   499в†’```bash
   500в†’# GOOD
   501в†’tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   502в†’tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   503в†’
   504в†’# BAD
   505в†’tasks/todo.md
   506в†’tasks/task1.md
   507в†’```
   508в†’
   509в†’## 7. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   510в†’
   511в†’```bash
   512в†’# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   513в†’# - Hotfix (site down)
   514в†’# - Revert broken commit
   515в†’# - Critical security fix
   516в†’
   517в†’# NOT for:
   518в†’# - "I'm lazy"
   519в†’# - "I'll update task file later"
   520в†’```
   521в†’
   522в†’---
   523в†’
   524в†’## Related Documentation
   525в†’
   526в†’- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
   527в†’- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
   528в†’- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
   529в†’- `.claude/commands/` - Custom commands
   530в†’
   531в†’---
   532в†’

   534в†’**Last Updated:** December 2025
   535в†’
   536в†’**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development.
   537в†’

# Workflow Automation Guide

**Version:** 4.0 (Consolidated)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Complete workflow compliance guide - checklists + automation tools

---

## Quick Navigation

| Section | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [Quick Checklists](#quick-checklists) | Pre-flight, implementation, completion |
| [Automation Tools](#automation-tools) | Git hooks, templates, commands |
| [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions) | Initial configuration |
| [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) | Common issues and fixes |
| [Customization](#customization) | Extend and modify tools |
| [Best Practices](#best-practices) | Tips for success |

---

# PART 1: Quick Checklists

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules and core philosophy)
- [ ] **Sync with remote branch:**
  ```bash
  git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit
  # OR: git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
  ```
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible**
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes
- [ ] **Follow privacy/security/UX principles**

---

## Project-Type Checklists

### For UI/Visual Changes

- [ ] Screenshot current state before changes
- [ ] Make ONE change at a time
- [ ] Test in browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] Test responsiveness: 375px, 768px, 1024px+
- [ ] Compare to baseline (visual diff)
- [ ] Check animations work (60fps target)
- [ ] Get user approval before committing

### For Node.js/React/TypeScript Projects

- [ ] Run tests: `npm run test`
- [ ] Type check: `npm run type-check`
- [ ] Lint check: `npm run lint`
- [ ] Build check: `npm run build`
- [ ] Verify bundle size acceptable

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

- [ ] Manual verification in browser
- [ ] Check console for errors
- [ ] Resize window (mobile/desktop)
- [ ] Run Lighthouse audit in DevTools

### For Automation/Scripts

- [ ] Test with `DRY_RUN=true` first
- [ ] Verify output matches expectations
- [ ] Check validation rules work
- [ ] Test edge cases
- [ ] Update docs if behavior changes

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md`
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed
- [ ] **Verify in light mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Verify in dark mode** (if applicable)
- [ ] **Test keyboard navigation** (accessibility)
- [ ] **Test at different screen sizes**

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Stage task file:** `git add tasks/[feature].md`
- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff --staged`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why

  Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore

---

## Quick Reference Tables

### Core Workflow Rules

| # | Rule | Description |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | **Think в†’ Plan в†’ Write** | Create task file FIRST before coding |
| 2 | **Plan has todo list** | Checklist with items to check off |
| 3 | **Check before working** | Get user approval via ExitPlanMode |
| 4 | **Mark tasks complete** | Update todos as you go |
| 5 | **High-level explanations** | Don't overexplain every detail |
| 6 | **Simple changes** | Minimal code impact |
| 7 | **Add review section** | Document what changed when done |
| 8 | **No lazy fixes** | Find root cause, no temporary patches |
| 9 | **Simplicity is key** | Impact minimal code, avoid bugs |
| 10 | **Update documentation** | Keep docs detailed, clear, clean |
| 11 | **Sync with remote** | Start of EVERY session |
| 12 | **Perfect foundation** | Build for 100% even if 1% complete |
| 13 | **Complete logical units** | Never stop mid-implementation |
| 14 | **Validate current date** | Check `<env>` section |
| 15 | **Commit & Push at end** | Git add, commit with details, push |

### Core Philosophy Principles

| # | Principle | Description |
|---|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | **Correctness & Integrity** | No data loss. Security P0. |
| 2 | **Coherence** | Follow existing patterns. No competing paradigms. |
| 3 | **Atomic Execution** | Small, reviewable steps. Explain WHY. |
| 4 | **Maintainability** | Explicit > Clever. No magic. |
| 5 | **Optimal Location** | Files must be in the most logical place. |
| 6 | **Scalable Architecture** | Build primitives, not one-offs. |
| 7 | **Root Cause Only** | Trace bugs to source. No band-aids. |
| 8 | **Infinite Initiative** | Fix broken windows immediately. |

---

# PART 2: Automation Tools

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:

- **Preventing commits** without task file updates
- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation
- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks
- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

### Supported Project Types

| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
|--------------|------------|-------------|
| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |

---

## Tool 1: Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

## Tool 2: Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- `tasks/refactor-state-management.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- `tasks/todo.md`
- `tasks/task1.md`
- `tasks/stuff.md`

---

## Tool 3: Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`

### `/start-task` Command
- Routes to appropriate workflow based on task type
- Creates task files from templates
- Checks existing todos
- Initiates planning mode
- Gets user approval before implementation

### `/run` Command
- Execute prompts with auto-archiving

---

# PART 3: Setup Instructions

## File Structure

```
[project-root]/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .git/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ hooks/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                   (global instructions)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.md                   (folder overview)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docs/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflow-automation.md  (this file)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ task-template.md        (task file template)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ commands/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ start-task.md           (custom commands)
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ skills/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tasks/                          (task files go here)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                       (main workflow guide)
в””в”Ђв”Ђ README.md                       (project documentation)
```

## Initial Setup

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Make hook executable (if needed)
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

## For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small test change
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

# PART 4: Troubleshooting

## Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:** Can commit code without task file

**Fixes:**

```bash
# Cause 1: Hook not executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Cause 2: Hook file doesn't exist
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Cause 3: Using --no-verify flag (don't do this)
git commit  # Correct
git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
```

## Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:** Hook blocks even with task file staged

**Fixes:**

```bash
# Cause 1: Task file in wrong location
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
git add my-feature.md         # Wrong

# Cause 2: Task file not staged
git diff --cached --name-only  # Check what's staged

# Cause 3: Wrong file extension
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct (.md)
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong
```

## Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd
```

---

# PART 5: Customization

## Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Add more directories to monitor
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

## Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add sections like:
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps

## Accessibility Checklist
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Screen reader compatible
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
```

## Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

```markdown
---
description: Your command description
---

# Command Content Here
```

---

# PART 6: Best Practices

## 1. Always Start with the Checklist

```bash
# View this file or use command
/start-task
```

## 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

## 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked
```

## 4. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

## 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# WRONG - Hook will block
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
```

## 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# GOOD
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md

# BAD
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
```

## 7. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
# - Hotfix (site down)
# - Revert broken commit
# - Critical security fix

# NOT for:
# - "I'm lazy"
# - "I'll update task file later"
```

---

## Related Documentation

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom commands

---

**Version:** 4.0 (Consolidated from workflow-checklist.md + automation-guide.md)
**Last Updated:** December 2025

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development.

# Plans Folder вЂ” Organization Guidelines

> **Purpose:** Central repository for project plans stored outside their source codebases.
> **When to use:** Plans that apply to projects where you don't want to clutter the project repo, or cross-project planning documents.

---

## Folder Structure

```
plans/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md              # This file
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ neuman-brain/          # Neuman Brain productivity platform
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ oakstone-properties/   # OakStone rental property website
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ thedeadrobot/          # TDR Portfolio / X automation
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lazy-golfing/          # Lazy Golfing platform (LZG)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ notes-migration/       # Personal notes conversion projects
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ claude-config/         # .claude folder optimization
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ meta/                  # Cross-project or meta-planning docs
в””в”Ђв”Ђ [new-project]/         # Create as needed
```

---

## File Placement Rules

### When Adding a New Plan

1. **Identify the project** from the plan's content (look for project names, file paths, feature references)
2. **Place in existing folder** if project folder exists
3. **Create new folder** if project is new (use kebab-case: `project-name/`)
4. **Use `meta/`** for cross-project or general planning documents

### Project Identification Hints

| Keywords/Paths in Document | Project Folder |
|---------------------------|----------------|
| `Neuman Brain`, `useStore`, `widgets`, `Dashboard`, `src/pages` | `neuman-brain/` |
| `OakStone`, `rental`, `property`, `Astro`, `Decap CMS` | `oakstone-properties/` |
| `thedeadrobot`, `TDR`, `X automation`, `Post-to-X`, `GitHub Actions` | `thedeadrobot/` |
| `LZG`, `Lazy Golfing`, `rounds`, `golf`, `lzghome`, `lzgbackend` | `lazy-golfing/` |
| `Notability`, `.note`, `OneNote`, `notebooks migration` | `notes-migration/` |
| `.claude/`, `skills`, `CLAUDE.md optimization`, `context reduction` | `claude-config/` |
| Cross-project, task inventory, general workflow | `meta/` |

---

## Naming Conventions

### Plan Files
- Use the auto-generated creative names (e.g., `velvet-brewing-sun.md`)
- Or use descriptive names: `feature-name-plan.md`, `phase-2-implementation.md`
- Prefix with date if time-sensitive: `2025-12-feature-plan.md`

### Project Folders
- Use kebab-case: `project-name/`
- Match the project's common reference name
- Keep names short but recognizable

---

## When to Store Plans Here vs. In Project Repo

### Store HERE (in `.claude/plans/`) when:
- Plan is exploratory and may not be implemented
- You want to keep the project repo clean
- Plan references multiple external systems
- Plan is for personal reference, not team collaboration

### Store IN PROJECT REPO when:
- Plan will be actively tracked by the team
- Plan relates to a specific PR or feature branch
- Plan needs version control alongside code changes
- Project has its own `tasks/` or `.planning/` directory

---

## Maintenance

### Quarterly Review
- Archive completed plans to `~/.claude/.archive/plans/`
- Update project folder list if new projects added
- Consolidate related plans if they've grown stale

### When a Project Completes
- Move plans to `~/.claude/.archive/plans/`
- Or delete if no longer needed for reference

### Archive Location
All archived content goes to the centralized `~/.claude/.archive/` folder:
- `~/.claude/.archive/plans/` - Completed plan files
- `~/.claude/.archive/project-*` - Historical project snapshots

---

## Quick Reference: Current Projects

| Folder | Description | Active? |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `neuman-brain/` | Personal productivity platform (React/TypeScript) | Yes |
| `oakstone-properties/` | Rental property website (Astro/Tailwind) | Yes |
| `thedeadrobot/` | Portfolio + X automation | Yes |
| `lazy-golfing/` | Golf platform (Next.js) | Yes |
| `notes-migration/` | One-time migration project | Paused |
| `claude-config/` | This .claude folder optimization | Ongoing |
| `meta/` | General/cross-project docs | Always |
